title: When status changes to something, assign person who made the change
excerpt: I’d like to be able to setup an automation to do the following… when status changes to something, assign the person who made the change to a person column.  This would simplify identifying who is working on specific task…

posts.author: RLMyrick (Rebecca Myrick)
posts.cookedHtml: I’d like to be able to setup an automation to do the following… when status changes to something, assign the person who made the change to a person column. This would simplify identifying who is working on specific tasks, or performing specific functions on a task.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Do you have Pro or Enterprise? This is something that can be done with the workflow center.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
If you are open to an app, check out Column Magic - monday.com Marketplace.

It contains this recipe:

image2068×302 21.2 KB

Jim - The Monday Man
Get Custom Apps, Integrations & Automations for monday

posts.author: RLMyrick (Rebecca Myrick)
posts.cookedHtml: I think I have Enterprise. How would I do it via workflows? I explored workflows a bit and couldn’t see how to get it to identify the person who’d made the update.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
It would be the ‘Triggered by’ as the person you’re assigning.

Hope that helps!

image1478×609 46.6 KB

posts.author: riyaz8501 (Riyaz)
posts.cookedHtml: You can achieve this by setting up an automation or workflow rule: when the status changes, use a trigger to automatically assign the task to the user who made the change

posts.author: RLMyrick (Rebecca Myrick)
posts.cookedHtml: I hadn’t seen that! Thanks so much!
